title: Microsoft And DeepSeek Are Launching New AI Together
summary: Microsoft is making Copilot Cowork generally available with usage-based billing and considering DeepSeek as a lower-cost model option on Azure. The shift represents a strategic move from single-assistant to multi-model AI agent platform capable of routing enterprise work across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork, and DeepSeek models.
writeup: The video covers Microsoft's move to make Copilot Cowork generally available with usage-based billing, alongside reported consideration of DeepSeek as a lower-cost model option on Azure. It matters because it signals a shift from a single-assistant approach to a multi-model AI agent platform that routes enterprise work across OpenAI, Claude, Cowork, and DeepSeek models. The key takeaways: Cowork's general availability paired with consumption pricing, and model routing as the mechanism for assigning enterprise tasks across multiple providers rather than relying on one assistant.
